# Svtter's Blog

> 在你想要放弃的时候，想想你为什么开始

## About

This is a technical blog maintained by Svtter, covering topics in software development, DevOps practices, and machine learning.

The blog is built with Hugo and the Stack theme, featuring articles on:
- Python programming and best practices
- DevOps tools and workflows (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD)
- Machine learning concepts and implementations
- Software engineering thoughts and methodologies

## Recent Posts

- [OMP M3 Model Patch: Adding MiniMax M3 to pi-ai](https://svtter.cn/en/p/omp-m3-model-patch-adding-minimax-m3-to-pi-ai/): How to add MiniMax M3 model support to the bundled models.json in @oh-my-pi/pi-ai@15.7.3 before upstream ships an official entry.
- [How kimi-code Handles kimi-k2.6: A Comparison with OpenCode](https://svtter.cn/en/p/how-kimi-code-handles-kimi-k2.6-a-comparison-with-opencode/): Recently, kimi-code migrated from Python to TypeScript. Here's a quick analysis.
- [RoboOmp: An AI Bot That Creates Its Own Pull Requests](https://svtter.cn/en/p/roboomp-an-ai-bot-that-creates-its-own-pull-requests/): Yesterday at the Oh My Pi (OMP) repository, I experienced something shocking: an AI bot didn&rsquo;t just reply to my issue—it understood the problem, …
- [OpenCode's GitHub Actions Automation System: Engineering Practices Behind 27 Workflows](https://svtter.cn/en/p/opencodes-github-actions-automation-system-engineering-practices-behind-27-workflows/): Analyzing 27 GitHub Actions workflows in the opencode project, breaking down automation strategies for CI/CD, community governance, and AI integration, along with insights for open source projects.
- [OpenCode Optimization Beyond Configuration — Plugin-Based Optimization](https://svtter.cn/en/p/opencode-optimization-beyond-configuration-plugin-based-optimization/): Starting from the development motivation of opencode-review, this article discusses code review issues in the AI coding era, the design rationale for multi-dimensional analysis, and the academic foundations of automatic fix chains.
- [A New Blog Format](https://svtter.cn/en/p/a-new-blog-format/): Recently, I explored the development trajectory of DeepSeek and compiled a DeepSeek Technical Evolution. After reviewing it, I found it to be a …
- [sth: An HTML Preview Server for AI Agents](https://svtter.cn/en/p/sth-an-html-preview-server-for-ai-agents/): I&rsquo;ve open sourced a small tool: static-html, with the command-line name sth.
What it does is simple: it provides an HTTP service that lets you …
- [DeepSeek + Claude Code: Thinking Block Compatibility Analysis](https://svtter.cn/en/p/deepseek--claude-code-thinking-block-compatibility-analysis/): Analysis of the root cause of 400 errors triggered in multi-turn conversations when Claude Code uses DeepSeek models in extended thinking mode, comparison with OpenCode's handling approach, and community solutions.
- [How to Fix DeepSeek Model Reasoning Issues in OpenCode](https://svtter.cn/en/p/how-to-fix-deepseek-model-reasoning-issues-in-opencode/): Troubleshooting and resolving the "reasoning_content must be passed back to the API" error when using opencode with deepseek-reasoner.
- [opencode-actions - A Code Review Agent](https://svtter.cn/en/p/opencode-actions-a-code-review-agent/): A GitHub Action built on opencode that provides automated code review and runner execution capabilities, supporting Z.AI, ZHIPU, and OpenCode Go subscriptions.
- [I'm Writing Articles Myself Again, and Some Thoughts on GPT-5.4](https://svtter.cn/en/p/im-writing-articles-myself-again-and-some-thoughts-on-gpt-5.4/): I&rsquo;ve gone back to writing articles &ldquo;myself&rdquo; again. The reason I say &ldquo;myself&rdquo; is:
Actually, my recent articles were all …
- [Does Self-Hosting an LLM Really Let You Use It Without Limits?](https://svtter.cn/en/p/does-self-hosting-an-llm-really-let-you-use-it-without-limits/): Self-hosting an LLM does not magically remove constraints. The real question is where the breakeven point sits between hardware, operations, and API token costs.
- [The Mathematical Trap of Big Model Coding Plan Packages: Can Promised Usage Be Delivered Under Concurrency Limits?](https://svtter.cn/en/p/the-mathematical-trap-of-big-model-coding-plan-packages-can-promised-usage-be-delivered-under-concurrency-limits/): Calculating the actual usable volume of big model Coding packages from the perspective of concurrency limits and throughput, revealing the gap between promises and reality.
- [Private Cloud for Small Companies](https://svtter.cn/en/p/private-cloud-for-small-companies/): Many friends want to know: What is the internal server situation and infrastructure of our small software development company?
Internal development …
- [Recently Discovered Useful MCP Tools](https://svtter.cn/en/p/recently-discovered-useful-mcp-tools/): Can be used for opencode / claude code
- [Efficient and Cost-Effective: My AI Agent Workflow Choice](https://svtter.cn/en/p/efficient-and-cost-effective-my-ai-agent-workflow-choice/): Sharing my efficient and cost-effective workflow using OpenCode + Gemini 3 Flash
- [Coding Performance and Model Cost-Effectiveness Analysis](https://svtter.cn/en/p/coding-performance-and-model-cost-effectiveness-analysis/): How to choose programming models in January 2026.
- [Configuring Claude Code Python UV Hooks and Skills](https://svtter.cn/en/p/configuring-claude-code-python-uv-hooks-and-skills/): Troubleshooting Guide: Using Hooks to Force Claude Code to Use uv for Python Dependency Management
- [Running Claude Code in a VM for Automated Testing and Development](https://svtter.cn/en/p/running-claude-code-in-a-vm-for-automated-testing-and-development/): Recently, I verified an effective AI development method that doesn&rsquo;t affect the existing workflow. Here&rsquo;s a summary.
Why Choose VM + …
- [Hugo Admin - A Lightweight Hugo Blog Management Interface](https://svtter.cn/en/p/hugo-admin-a-lightweight-hugo-blog-management-interface/): When managing a Hugo blog, you often need to switch back and forth between the terminal and editor. To simplify this process, I developed hugo-admin, …
